jokes.text: Come to Philly for the crack - It has a picture of the Liberty Bell on it
jokes.setup: Charlie defending his t-shirt as funny and original
jokes.punchline: The double meaning of 'crack' - Liberty Bell's literal crack vs. crack cocaine
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that establishes Charlie's oblivious personality and questionable taste. Economical and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Come to Philly for the crack - It has a picture of the Liberty Bell on it

jokes.text: Well, excuse me if all my T-shirts don't have a little guy playing polo on the lapel
jokes.setup: Dennis critiquing Charlie's fashion choices
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sarcastic defense reveals his class resentment and misunderstanding of polo shirts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's defensive insecurity about class differences.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, excuse me if all my T-shirts don't have a little guy playing polo on the lapel

jokes.text: They're tribal - Oh, they're trib... I'm sorry. What tribe are you from? What? Is it, like, an Indian tribe or...
jokes.setup: Mac defending his tattoos as 'tribal'
jokes.punchline: Dennis exposing the cultural appropriation absurdity by asking what tribe
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary through character interaction. Dennis's deadpan delivery makes Mac's pretension absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: They're tribal

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, they're trib... I'm sorry. What tribe are you from? What? Is it, like, an Indian tribe or...

jokes.text: Are you gonna turn into the mad munchkin? Do you represent the Lollipop Guild, Charlie?
jokes.setup: Charlie wanting to play instead of ref
jokes.punchline: Height-based insults comparing Charlie to Wizard of Oz munchkins
jokes.explanation: Predictable short joke but works for character dynamics and leads to physical comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Are you gonna turn into the mad munchkin? Do you represent the Lollipop Guild, Charlie?

jokes.text: It's about our son!
jokes.setup: Woman approaching Charlie privately
jokes.punchline: Bomb drop about paternity in public setting
jokes.explanation: Effective plot bomb that creates immediate awkwardness and sets up episode conflict.

dialogue.line: It's about our son!

jokes.text: Nobody wants to hear that
jokes.setup: Stacy's loud declaration about Charlie's son
jokes.punchline: Dennis's understated response to the public drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that punctures the drama with social awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Nobody wants to hear that

jokes.text: I mean, I wish I could go back in time and do the right thing, you know? - Like be there for him? - No. Get her an abortion.
jokes.setup: Charlie expressing regret about not doing 'the right thing'
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation of responsibility with selfish abortion wish
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that reveals Charlie's selfishness. Dark humor executed with excellent timing and character truth.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I mean, I wish I could go back in time and do the right thing, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Like be there for him?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No. Get her an abortion.

jokes.text: So you're not allowed to use birth control, but abortions are no problem?
jokes.setup: Charlie's Catholic school excuse followed by abortion comment
jokes.punchline: Dee pointing out the logical inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about religious hypocrisy that lands well.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: So you're not allowed to use birth control, but abortions are no problem?

jokes.text: You remember Genesis, Book Two, Verse Three? And he breathed into the nostrils of Adam on the first day. And it was good.
jokes.setup: Mac claiming biblical authority
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up biblical quote with ridiculous specificity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Mac's fake religious authority through absurd biblical invention.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You remember Genesis, Book Two, Verse Three? And he breathed into the nostrils of Adam on the first day. And it was good.

jokes.text: Right in his nostrils, huh? Sounds really uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Mac's fake biblical quote about breathing into nostrils
jokes.punchline: Dennis's literal interpretation focuses on the physical discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that deflates Mac's pretension with literal interpretation.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Right in his nostrils, huh? Sounds really uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I remember Stacy Corvelli. She was a slut.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting a blood test
jokes.punchline: Mac's crude assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Crude character moment that's cheap but fits Mac's personality.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I remember Stacy Corvelli. She was a slut.

jokes.text: Uh, Mac, what's God's stance on blood tests? Hmm. I don't know. I'd have to check.
jokes.setup: Mac's religious obsession
jokes.punchline: Mac needing to consult on absurdly mundane medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Mac's ridiculous religious extremism.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, Mac, what's God's stance on blood tests?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hmm. I don't know. I'd have to check.

jokes.text: Which one of you fags is supposed to be my dad?
jokes.setup: First meeting between Charlie and potential son
jokes.punchline: Kid immediately being hostile and using slurs
jokes.explanation: Shock value comedy that establishes the kid's awful personality.

dialogue.line: Which one of you fags is supposed to be my dad?

jokes.text: Are you serious? This is the guy?
jokes.setup: Tommy seeing Charlie for first time
jokes.punchline: Kid's immediate disappointment in his potential father
jokes.explanation: Good reaction comedy showing mutual disappointment.

dialogue.line: Are you serious? This is the guy?

jokes.text: I'll blow you for $10. You know what? Let me consult some of this free literature they got here regarding that proposition.
jokes.setup: Prostitute offering services at clinic
jokes.punchline: Dennis treating it like a business decision requiring research
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Dennis's pretentious nature even in crude situations.

dialogue.speaker: Prostitute
dialogue.line: I'll blow you for $10.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You know what? Let me consult some of this free literature they got here regarding that proposition.

jokes.text: I wanna go to the mall! He's biting my hand! He's biting my hand!
jokes.setup: Kid demanding to go to mall
jokes.punchline: Escalates to physical violence with biting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie's incompetence with children.

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: I wanna go to the mall!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's biting my hand! He's biting my hand!

jokes.text: I don't really wanna join your little freak show. Just looking for some information.
jokes.setup: Mac at anti-abortion organization
jokes.punchline: Calling the group a 'freak show' while seeking their help
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Mac's dismissive attitude toward his own cause.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't really wanna join your little freak show. Just looking for some information.

jokes.text: I hate dead baby fetuses, you know? I hate them because they're dead, and they shouldn't be. They should be alive, and they should be loved.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to show passion for the cause
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic of 'hating' dead fetuses as if it's their fault
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic that shows Mac's complete misunderstanding of his own position.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I hate dead baby fetuses, you know? I hate them because they're dead, and they shouldn't be. They should be alive, and they should be loved.

jokes.text: Charlie, what in the hell are you gonna do if this kid's yours? Oh, I don't know. I'll probably, uh, kill myself.
jokes.setup: Dee asking about Charlie's plans if paternity is confirmed
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual mention of suicide as solution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually, perfectly capturing Charlie's avoidance and desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Charlie, what in the hell are you gonna do if this kid's yours?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, I don't know. I'll probably, uh, kill myself.

jokes.text: That is such a coincidence. That's exactly what we're here doing.
jokes.setup: Waitress mentioning Big Sister program
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately lying to create connection
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the deception plot showing Dee's willingness to help Charlie's schemes.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: That is such a coincidence. That's exactly what we're here doing.

jokes.text: He loves Mexicans.
jokes.setup: Trying to make Charlie seem good with the Mexican girl
jokes.punchline: Horribly awkward and potentially racist statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing how their attempts to be helpful backfire spectacularly.

dialogue.line: He loves Mexicans.

jokes.text: That's the list of doctors I'm gonna kill. There's two already crossed out.
jokes.setup: Mac asking about being hard-core
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of murder list with evidence of success
jokes.explanation: Extremely dark humor that escalates perfectly and shows the extremist character's true nature.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's the list of doctors I'm gonna kill. There's two already crossed out.

jokes.text: I can't do this! Tommy's not your son. RememberJimmy Doyle from high school? That son of a bitch is Tommy's real father.
jokes.setup: Charlie finally bonding with responsibility
jokes.punchline: Complete rug pull revealing the deception
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist that subverts audience expectations and Charlie's character growth.

dialogue.line: I can't do this! Tommy's not your son. RememberJimmy Doyle from high school? That son of a bitch is Tommy's real father.

jokes.text: God! All right. So can I still take him out on Saturday?
jokes.setup: Learning Tommy isn't his son and the mother's deception
jokes.punchline: Charlie still wanting to keep the kid despite everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Charlie's bizarre priorities and attachment issues.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: God! All right. So can I still take him out on Saturday?

jokes.text: I don't really have any convictions.
jokes.setup: Dee asking if he'll abandon convictions to get laid
jokes.punchline: Dennis admitting he has no real beliefs
jokes.explanation: Perfect character revelation delivered with casual honesty that's both funny and revealing.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't really have any convictions.

jokes.text: I will smash your face into a... into a jelly!
jokes.setup: Charlie bribing Tommy to be good
jokes.punchline: Kid's violent threat with weird food metaphor
jokes.explanation: Funny violent threat from kid with absurd imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Tommy
dialogue.line: I will smash your face into a... into a jelly!

jokes.text: Get lost, breeder.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to hit on pro-choice activist
jokes.punchline: Being dismissed with derogatory term for straight people
jokes.explanation: Good rejection that highlights Dennis's misreading of the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Pro-choice woman
dialogue.line: Get lost, breeder.

jokes.text: Are you hitting on me at an abortion rally?
jokes.setup: Dennis awkwardly trying to flirt
jokes.punchline: Woman calling out the inappropriate timing and location
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation about inappropriate behavior that creates good cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Pro-choice woman
dialogue.line: Are you hitting on me at an abortion rally?

jokes.text: I think they can just smell how disgusting you are.
jokes.setup: Dennis wondering if women are gay
jokes.punchline: Kid's brutal assessment of Dennis's failure
jokes.explanation: Savage comeback from kid that deflates Dennis's ego perfectly.

dialogue.line: I think they can just smell how disgusting you are.

jokes.text: You gotta get an abortion.
jokes.setup: Megan revealing she's pregnant
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately suggesting abortion despite his pro-life stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy moment showing Mac's selfishness trumping his stated beliefs. Excellent character work.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You gotta get an abortion.

jokes.text: You can't have this kid. I'm way too young, and I have a little bit of an alcohol problem...
jokes.setup: Mac trying to convince Megan to abort
jokes.punchline: Admitting personal problems while being selfish
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's selfishness and self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You can't have this kid. I'm way too young, and I have a little bit of an alcohol problem...

jokes.text: This kid reeks of booze! Are you drunk? Don't be drunk.
jokes.setup: Tommy being in the bar
jokes.punchline: Kid getting drunk and Charlie's panicked response
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing complete parenting failure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This kid reeks of booze! Are you drunk? Don't be drunk.

jokes.text: Maybe that's why you don't have a dad anymore!
jokes.setup: Charlie frustrated with drunk kid's behavior
jokes.punchline: Cruel comment about kid's abandonment issues
jokes.explanation: Dark moment showing Charlie's complete lack of parenting instincts and empathy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Maybe that's why you don't have a dad anymore!

jokes.text: Why? Why?
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to have serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Jimmy doing the same annoying 'why' game as his son
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing where the kid gets his annoying behavior, creating satisfying symmetry.

dialogue.line: Why? Why?
